plan.goal: Address code review findings for Phase 1: fix validation gaps, override handling bugs, schema strictness, and misleading comments
plan.context:
This is the `wf` workflow orchestrator project. Phase 1 implemented pure foundation modules (types.py, config.py, brief.py, etc.). A code review found 7 findings; we're addressing all except #6 (brief path normalization/rationale comments — already tracked in implementation-tracker.md, blocked on Phase 2/5 dependencies).

Key files:
- `wflib/types.py`: Dataclasses, serialization/deserialization, `validate_schema()`, `plan_from_json()`, `record_from_json()`
- `wflib/config.py`: Config loading, merging, resolution, `parse_overrides()`, `apply_cli_overrides()`, `resolve_config()`
- `schemas/workflow.schema.json`: JSON Schema with `additionalProperties: false` on sub-schemas
- `tests/unit/test_types.py` and `tests/unit/test_config.py`: Existing test suites

The spec says (line 188): "A thin validation layer checks required keys exist, values have the right types, and arrays meet minimum lengths before construction." The spec also says (line 745): "Sub-schemas DO use additionalProperties: false because they are tool registration contracts — strict validation catches malformed LLM tool calls." The root WorkflowRecord intentionally does NOT use additionalProperties: false for forward-compat.

tasks.title: Add additionalProperties enforcement and type strictness to validate_schema
tasks.goal: Make `validate_schema` enforce `additionalProperties: false` (reject extra keys) and ensure all type checks work correctly. This addresses findings #1 (partially) and #2. When a schema object has `additionalProperties: false`, any keys in the data not listed in `properties` should produce an error. When `additionalProperties` is a schema (like `{"type": "string"}`), validate values against that schema. Also add `plan_from_json()` integration: it should call `validate_schema(data, 'plan')` before deserializing, so malformed LLM tool-call payloads are caught. `record_from_json()` should NOT be changed — it intentionally tolerates unknown top-level keys for forward-compat.
constraints: Only enforce additionalProperties on objects where the schema explicitly sets it — don't assume it
When additionalProperties is false, report each extra key as a separate error with its path
When additionalProperties is a schema (e.g. {"type": "string"}), validate values against that schema
plan_from_json() should call validate_schema(data, 'plan') and raise ValueError with joined errors if validation fails, BEFORE calling _dict_to_dataclass
record_from_json() must NOT be changed — it already handles forward-compat by filtering to known fields
Do not add type checking to _dict_to_dataclass/_deserialize_value — keep the validation in validate_schema
acceptance: validate_schema(data, 'plan') returns errors for extra keys when additionalProperties is false
validate_schema(data, 'plan') returns errors for wrong-typed values (e.g. integer where string expected)
validate_schema with additionalProperties as a schema (e.g. ImplementationRecord.tasks, ImplementationRecord.activeResources) validates values correctly
plan_from_json raises ValueError when given a payload with extra keys or wrong types
record_from_json still accepts records with unknown top-level keys (forward-compat preserved)
All existing tests in tests/unit/test_types.py still pass
New tests cover: extra keys rejected, wrong types rejected, additionalProperties-as-schema validated, plan_from_json strict validation

tasks.title: Fix override handling: coerce types, validate CLI overrides, enforce format
tasks.goal: Fix findings #3, #4, and #5 in config.py. (1) `parse_overrides()` should enforce `section.key` format and normalize dash-case keys to underscore using the existing `_DASH_TO_UNDERSCORE` map. (2) `resolve_config()` should coerce override string values to native types using `_coerce_string_value` before validation. (3) `apply_cli_overrides()` should validate values using the same `_VALIDATION_RULES` registry before applying them, raising ConfigError on invalid values.
constraints: parse_overrides() must require exactly one dot in the key (section.key format) — raise ValueError for non-dotted keys with a clear message like "Override key must be in 'section.key' format: 'concurrency'"
parse_overrides() must normalize dash-case subkeys using _DASH_TO_UNDERSCORE (e.g. 'execute.auto-review' -> 'execute.auto_review')
In resolve_config(), apply _coerce_string_value to each leaf value in overrides_dict before calling _validate_values, iterating the nested dict structure (section -> key -> string value)
apply_cli_overrides() must validate each override value using _VALIDATION_RULES before applying it, raising ConfigError on invalid values (e.g. concurrency=0, concurrency=-1)

The existing test_parse_overrides test 'test_simple_key_value' expects parse_overrides(['concurrency=8']) to return {'concurrency': '8'} — this test must be UPDATED to expect a ValueError since non-dotted keys are now rejected
acceptance: parse_overrides(['concurrency=8']) raises ValueError mentioning 'section.key' format
parse_overrides(['execute.auto-review=false']) returns {'execute': {'auto_review': 'false'}} (dash normalized)
resolve_config(cwd, overrides=['execute.concurrency=8']) succeeds and returns config with concurrency=8
resolve_config(cwd, overrides=['ui.tmux=false']) succeeds and returns config with tmux=False
resolve_config(cwd, overrides=['execute.concurrency=abc']) raises ConfigError
apply_cli_overrides(config, concurrency=0) raises ConfigError
apply_cli_overrides(config, concurrency=-1) raises ConfigError
apply_cli_overrides(config, concurrency=4) succeeds
All existing tests pass (with the one test updated for the new format enforcement)
New tests cover: non-dotted key rejection, dash normalization, numeric/bool override coercion, CLI validation

tasks.title: Fix misleading dict key comment in _deserialize_value
tasks.goal: Fix finding #7: the comment in `_deserialize_value` at the dict handling section says 'Convert camelCase keys back to snake_case for dict fields that use string keys matching Python identifiers' but the code does `py_key = k` (keeps as-is). Fix the comment to accurately describe the behavior: dict keys are data keys (task IDs, alias names, profile names) and are preserved unchanged — only dataclass field names go through camelCase/snake_case conversion.
constraints: Only change the comment, not the code behavior — dict keys staying as-is is correct
Make the comment explain WHY keys stay as-is (they're data keys, not field names)
acceptance: The comment accurately describes the code's behavior
No code behavior changes
All existing tests still pass
